name: Dr. Danielle Thomas
bio:
Danielle Thomas is a Systems Scientist at Carnegie Mellon University in the Human-Computer Interaction Institute within the School of Computer Science. Her research focuses on increasing learning opportunities for students through advancement of human-computer tutoring and tutor training. Both interests focus on bringing opportunities to students—a passion she holds dear after over a decade of working in public education as a middle school teacher, academic coach, curriculum supervisor, and principal.

Since her recent transition into higher education, Danielle has published proceedings on human-computer tutoring in Artificial Intelligence in Education and Learning@Scale and has authored in journals, such as the International Journal of Research in Education and Science on the impact of middle school STEM programming on student learning. She holds Masters degrees in Chemistry and Educational Administration with a doctorate in Educational Leadership.

name: Dr. Emma Brunskill
bio:
Emma Brunskill is an associate (tenured) professor in the Computer Science Department at Stanford University whose lab is part of the Stanford AI Lab, the Stanford Statistical ML group, and AI Safety @Stanford. She was previously an assistant professor of computer science at Carnegie Mellon University.

Brunskill’s work has been honored by early faculty career awards (National Science Foundation, Office of Naval Research, Microsoft Research (1 of 7 worldwide) and her lab has received several best research paper nominations (CHI, EDMx3) and awards (UAI, RLDM, ITS) for their work on reinforcement learning foundations, algorithms, and applications to education and healthcare.
Shivang’s research has been published in various learning science and analytics conferences including AIED, LAK, and more. He is a graduate of the Masters in Education Technology program at CMU and holds a Bachelor of Computer Science Engineering degree from the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology.

name: Dr. Erin Gatz
bio: Dr. Erin Gatz is the head of partnerships for PLUS – Personalized Learning Squared. Prior to this role, Erin was the Director of Research and Impact with Remake Learning and a visiting researcher with the Human-Computer Interaction Institute at CMU. Erin is Founder of Prototype PGH, a 501(c)3 that aims to build gender and racial equity through workshops, tech access, and peer support. Erin has a BA from the University of Waterloo, MEd from the University of Toronto, and PhD in Learning Sciences and Policy from the University of Pittsburgh. She is a proud graduate of Brashear High School in Pittsburgh.

name: Dr. Ken Koedinger
bio:
Kenneth R. Koedinger is a professor of Human Computer Interaction and Psychology at Carnegie Mellon University. Dr. Koedinger has an M.S. in Computer Science, a Ph.D. in Cognitive Psychology, and experience teaching in an urban high school. His multidisciplinary background supports his research goals of understanding human learning and creating educational technologies that increase student achievement.

Koedinger directs LearnLab, which is the scientific arm of CMU’s Simon Initiative. He was a co-founder of CarnegieLearning, Inc., which has brought Cognitive Tutor based courses to millions of students since it was formed in 1998, and leads LearnLab, now the scientific arm of CMU’s Simon Initiative. Dr. Koedinger has authored over 250 peer-reviewed publications and has been a project investigator on over 45 grants. In 2017, he received the Hillman Professorship of Computer Science, and in 2018, he was recognized as a fellow of Cognitive Science.

name: Dr. Lee Branstetter
bio:
Lee Branstetter is a professor of economics and public policy at Carnegie Mellon University, where he directs the Future of Work initiative at CMU’s Block Center for Technology and Society. Branstetter is also a nonresident senior fellow at the Peterson Institute for International Economics and a research associate of the National Bureau of Economic Research. He served as a Senior Economist at the Council of Economic Advisers in 2011-2012.

Before moving to Carnegie Mellon, Branstetter was the Daniel Stanton Associate Professor of Business at Columbia Business School. Branstetter has a Ph.D. in Economics from Harvard University and a BA in Economics and Mathematical Methods in the Social Sciences (MMSS) from Northwestern University.

name: Dr. Vincent Aleven
bio: Vincent Aleven is a professor and director of the undergraduate program at Carnegie Mellon University’s Human–Computer Interaction Institute. Specializing in artificial intelligence, intelligent tutoring systems, and learning and metacognitive skills, his research goals are dedicated to advancing the science of how people interact and learn with adaptive, AI-based learning technologies. The co-creator of Cognitive Tutor and the Adaptivity Grid, Aleven has helped develop dynamic learning environments that leverage the strengths of humans and AI to support students’ personalized, self-regulated, and collaborative learning.

name: Shivang Gupta
bio:
Shivang Gupta is the Head of Product at Carnegie Mellon University in charge of daily operations for the PLUS program. Shivang has worked in education technology for a decade, leading product development teams in Hong Kong, India, Estonia, and the US. His work focuses on learning science implementation and bringing research outcomes to practice.

Shivang’s research has been published in various learning science and analytics conferences including AIED, LAK, and more. He is a graduate of the Masters in Education Technology program at CMU and holds a Bachelor of Computer Science Engineering degree from the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology.

name: Bill Guo
bio:
Bill Guo leads a team of 15 designers at PLUS, where he oversees product design, brand vision, and design operations.

With a background in designing digital products and services, Bill has extensive experience in user research, running workshops, and fostering design communities within start-up environments and consultancy projects.

Bill holds a Bachelor of Science degree in Human-Computer Interaction from Carnegie Mellon University (CMU). He is a founding member and former co-president of the User Experience Association (UXA) at CMU. His design research has been published in prominent learning science and HCI conferences, including EC-TEL, CSCL, and CHI.

name: Cindy Tipper
bio: Cindy Tipper is a Principal Research Programmer at Carnegie Mellon University, leading software development for the PLUS project as well as DataShop, the world's largest repository of learning interaction data.

name: Dr. Ashish Gurung
bio:
Ashish is a postdoctoral researcher working at the intersection of Learning Science and Technology. His primary research interest is in building systems that facilitate equitable access to learning opportunities by leveraging aspects of learning analytics, classroom orchestration, and teaching augmentation. Ashish's research has been published in various venues that focus on AI in Education, Learning Analytics, and Educational Data Mining. He holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science from Worcester Polytechnic Institute.

Ashish has been contributing to the development of EdTech platforms for more than a decade. He was the team lead and served as a system architect for the E-Paath system at OLE-Nepal. He then worked on various aspects of the ASSISTments platform as part of the research team for his Ph.D. This combination of having worked both in industry and academia gives him a unique perspective regarding the potential and limitations of various aspects of EdTech platforms. He joined the PLUS team upon completing his Ph.D. and is currently developing tools to augment tutor insights into student needs.

name: Hui Cheng
bio: Hui Cheng is the QA engineer for the PLUS program. She is responsible for the quality control of the PLUS application. She is also involved in data analysis for the PLUS project. Hui Cheng got her MSIS from University of Pittsburgh.

name: Jose Deleon
bio:
Software Engineer at Carnegie Mellon UniversityJose Deleon is a software engineer at Carnegie Mellon University, serving a unique role that combines development work in maintaining and improving the PLUS app, as well as working on-site with partner schools.

Jose is part of the graduating class of 2023 from the University of California, San Diego, earning an undergraduate degree in Mathematics-Computer Science.

name: Coco Jiang
bio: Coco is a current METALS student at CMU.
